displayTitle: A Clean Film Is Not a Clean Plate

beats.narration: Take a casting and squeeze it: hammer, roll, extrude. The part gets stronger; every surviving flaw is flattened thin, tight, and planar.

payload.title: WROUGHT PRODUCTS
payload.sub: Worked metal: stronger part, flatter flaws.

beats.narration: Each process signs its work: forging leaves laps and bursts, rolling leaves laminations and seams, extrusion tears chevrons down the centerline.
Radiography only sees a flaw that changes the metal path. A flat lamination hides from a straight beam; aligned, it prints a sharp dark line.

payload.heading: Orientation decides detectability
payload.note: FLAT TO BEAM → NO IMAGE · ALIGNED → DARK LINE

beats.narration: Match the method: laps, magnetic particle. Bursts, flakes, laminations: ultrasonics. Chevrons, shot broadside, print dark V's — radiography's rare wrought win.

payload.heading: Match the method to the flaw

beats.narration: Here's the rule: a clean film is not a clean plate. If the beam can't line up with the flaw, change the method.

payload.sub: If the beam can't align — change the method.
